source.title: Clubhouse Map Setup Guide (Siege X) - How To Setup and Defend ALL Sites
source.notes: Recorded on Siege X (post-rework Clubhouse). Generic ranked setups from creator 'Littleton', not pro-league lineups. Auto-generated captions have several garbled operator names — flagged inline where the transcript audio was ambiguous. Room/callout names are the creator's informal callouts. Compass directions added from R6 Trainer's labeled floor-plan blueprints (r6trainer.com), cross-referenced against the game's own compass-based room callouts; north is up on the map.

reinforcements.note: Reinforce regardless of whether the tunnel door is barricaded. Check the angle where your feet could be shot from the drone before standing to repel/hold.

reinforcements.location: Rotate Wall Between Armory and Church (double wall)
reinforcements.note: Some setups reinforce this. Creator prefers to keep it soft so defenders can hold the hatch drop angle from here instead.
Reinforce left and right, leave middle-left soft (especially if a mirror is played there) to head-hole the middle onto Main Stairs.
Reinforce left and right, leave middle-left soft.
Keeping this closed is the single biggest factor in winning basement.
Creator's non-standard preference: put the rotate in the middle rather than bottom-left — see rotates.

headHoles.note: Head holes here to fight onto Main Stairs. Top of this section is soft and can be shot over toward bottom of Main Stairs.
Kept short so it only reveals attacker ankles running toward Moto without giving a reciprocal prone sightline down the corridor from bottom Main Stairs.
Standing on the box here sees Four and bottom Main Stairs — only relevant while the Kitchen hatch stays closed. Also watch for a Banshee/Fenrir repelling upside-down onto the hatch at round start.

rotates.note: Connects Armory side to Church, holds the hatch drop angle, lets you rotate to Blue from back here if the Kitchen hatch is closed.

rotates.location: Small Rotate, Right Side Near Armory
rotates.note: Fight onto Main Stairs from here; quick out if attackers fuse/hard-breach Kitchen, or a fast dart to rotate up into Kitchen once that hatch opens.

Non-standard: most setups reinforce the middle/right wall and put the rotate bottom-left; this puts the rotate in the middle instead. A middle rotate can't be seen from the hatch (blocked by the AC unit); the bottom-left rotate can. Trade-off: gives attackers a slightly wider view into Church.
If the Blue hatch is open, don't go past the AC unit line or you'll be visible from the hatch.

hatches.note: One of the three basement hatches.
Keeping this closed makes the basement significantly easier to defend.
Not visible from the middle rotate position (AC unit blocks it); visible from the standard bottom-left rotate position.

gadgetPlacements.location: Either Wall of North Dirt Tunnel
gadgetPlacements.note: Wait for attackers placing hard breach charges, then throw an electroclaw during roughly the first 30 seconds. Afterward relocate the claw to cover the Kitchen hatch and Moto hatch.
Hide behind corners using the wooden barriers for concealment.

Source audio said 'a banshee, a fen rear' — maps to Melusi's Banshee and/or Fenrir's dread mine. Goo mines (Lesion) called out as not reliable enough here; prox mines 'probably okay.' Some info gadget considered mandatory regardless of barricade status.
Stand on the box using the red numbers as a placement guide for a cage/gadget trick — usable at round start.

gadgetPlacements.location: Blue, Platform Step and Oil Pit Steps
gadgetPlacements.note: Tuck goo mines on the platform step, hidden; mirror the play on the oil pit steps. Pair with a bulletproof camera/mirror on the wall and peek down each side while mines work.
Place the mirror at crouch height, not standing — standing height can't see up into the hatch, crouch height can.

gadgetPlacements.location: Anchor Pocket Near Blue/Dirt/Kitchen Hatch Overlap
gadgetPlacements.note: Holds Dirt, one of the best angles onto the Kitchen hatch on the site, and also holds into Blue.

Creates a vertical sightline down into the Freezer door, catching attackers dropping the Kitchen hatch. A second angle from the same spot covers the hatch if attackers approach via the window instead. Attackers can open the Logi hatch at any time.
SMG-11 angle holds Kitchen from above and also sees down Main Stairs and Kitchen Corridor.

gadgetPlacements.location: Gas Canister, Top of Main Stairs
gadgetPlacements.note: Shoot to fully block the stairwell for several seconds (brief burn at the end) — use to cover a retreat. A second canister exists near the bottom, into Moto.

operatorRecommendations.note: Electroclaw punishes early hard breach attempts on the Dirt Tunnel walls, then relocates to cover the Kitchen and Moto hatches.
Mandatory info presence in Dirt Tunnel whether or not it's barricaded — source audio ambiguous between these two.
Goo mines on the platform step and oil pit steps, paired with a crouch-height mirror on the Blue wall.

generalTips.note: Barricading Dirt Tunnel stops the team playing info there and lets attackers walk to the barricade for free, drone under the door, and frag defenders blind. Creator recommends leaving it open.
These are generic rank setups, copper to champ — not pro-level lineups.
On feet holes: aim at head/chest height, not the feet themselves.
Shoot the jukebox and radio at round start.
AKs Cabinet is a callout reference only — no longer standable-on since a past patch.

sites.workingName: Gym / Bedroom (CC / Gym / Construction / Logi / Master / Bathroom)

reinforcements.note: Mirrors a standard CC-defense-style setup, adapted for this site.
Part of the same CC-style setup.
Reinforced as part of the CC-style setup, though the creator later suggests skipping this in favor of the Master/Gym window wall instead if reinforcements are tight.
Reinforce and try to get at least some denial (Mute jammer if a battery is spare) — once this wall opens, the whole other half of the map is cut off.
Reinforced.
Fully reinforce both.

reinforcements.location: West Wall of Master (Gym Window Wall, alternative pick)
reinforcements.note: Creator reconsiders mid-video: skip the Top Red reinforcement and reinforce this wall instead, since attackers can otherwise tank bullets through the soft wall to shut down all of Master and deny the Master/gym-window/Condor anchor point.

headHoles.location: CC Anchor Wall (Covers Gym Window, Master Window, Third Window)
headHoles.note: A line of three windows watched from one spot. Needs something covering Red Stairs so no one creeps up on it.

rotates.note: Placed on the closer side (unlike a normal CC-style defense) to shave time rotating back to peek the breach or Garage — roughly 2 seconds faster.
Always place on this side of the wall so the mattress hides it from the Master window — the other side gets you killed the instant someone comes through.

hatches.note: Not reinforced. Standing near it once open is not automatically safe — attackers can see you from the roof drop-down angle. Retreat to a covering position and wait for the drop, or hold the drop from elsewhere if the breach wall is still closed.
Holds the billiard window from this hatch — only until attackers open the window itself, after which the position is no longer safe.

gadgetPlacements.note: Two spare batteries to trick a Thermite/Ace charge on the CC wall, then run to the jacuzzi breach to trick again there.
Punch open at least once early, even without planning to peek it — pressures attackers on Highway.
Needs an info gadget so attackers can't crouch-walk up undetected. Suggested: Melusi (Banshee), proximity mines, or barbed wire.
If playing Cache/Master solo with a coordinating teammate, put a Mag-NET here plus hold Top Red — a Mag-NET can't be played if the wall it's on opens first.
Hard to prox-mine effectively — stair geometry lets attackers crouch under the trigger range. A Melusi Banshee is a better pick; barbed wire further down the stairs also works.

operatorRecommendations.role: CC/Gym early trick + roam to jacuzzi breach
operatorRecommendations.note: Runs both the CC wall and jacuzzi breach batteries early in the round.
Mag-NET support if the Top Red reinforcement is skipped in favour of the Master/Gym window wall.
Banshee for both Red Stairs (CC side) and Main Stairs (gym site) to prevent crouch-walking pushes.

generalTips.note: This is the east side of the map. Below platinum you can get away with under-defending it; platinum and above, set it up properly.
CC is not a place to spend much time — grab a drone or two then fall back into the Cache stronghold.
Logi and Construction are both hard to hold past the first minute or two — delay, gather info, then retreat.
Castle barricade math: breaks after 9 hits; pre-hit it 8 times so a single punch during the round finishes it off.
Gas pipes on this site do damage (up to ~50) if shot at the right angle to direct spray toward attackers.
Preferred over CCTV/Cash — play/practice this site before that one.

sites.workingName: CCTV / Cash (Garage / Cash / Construction / Top Red)
callouts: Panel One / Panel Two / Panel Three

reinforcements.note: Imperative to win this site. Combine with cage tricks (the '1' and '2' markers indicate the middle of the doors below each wall) to punish early hard breach attempts on both sets of walls.
Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Top-Middle Wall of Cash (of three on Red)
reinforcements.note: Reinforced.
Reinforced with two, but expected to open eventually unless your team has an exceptional Bandit tricker — once open, attackers can shoot through the softened side to threaten the Rafters player.
Reinforced so a defender pushed from the Construction door has cover.
The 10th and final reinforcement of the 10 available across the whole map setup.

headHoles.location: South Walls of Garage (Head Holes, Not a Rotate)
headHoles.note: Deliberately head holes rather than a rotate. If the Rafters player dies, a rotate would let any attacker walk straight up; head holes force a Sledge/Ash/Buck-type breach or hard breach, channelling attackers through the Garage window instead, which can be held from the top of Red Stairs.
Peeks the Construction door from both sides.
Extra holes at the top, useful for throwing C4s and smokes over the wall toward the breach/default plant area.

rotates.location: Cash Rotate (Up High, Vault-Only When Standing)
rotates.note: Attackers can't pull through it from their side. Lets a defender in Cash vault flank a planter or the Cash-door holder unseen, or throw smokes toward the breach/plant. Check the breach angle before using it.

rotates.location: Top Red Rotate, Right Side of the Wall
rotates.note: Kept flush with the edge of the reinforcement to peek both sides of the Construction door from it.

hatches.note: Feet holes in Construction watch this — placed to see attackers' feet as they drop onto the floor, without them seeing you.
Opened deliberately so defenders can hold the Stock door area and drop down if pressured.

gadgetPlacements.note: Source audio rendered the name phonetically as 'aammy'/'a wii' — matched to Wamai given the described Mag-NET play. Place magnets along the doors below, one over the door and one on the next level along, spaced so a single grenade can't destroy multiple magnets at once.
Use the '1' and '2' door-center markers to line up a cage/gadget trick covering both walls below simultaneously.

gadgetPlacements.location: Construction Door (anchor, behind the green desk)
gadgetPlacements.note: Covers Condor, CC window, and the breach, plus a prone angle on anyone coming up Red Stairs.
Opening the hatch lets you watch the walk-in from Stock and see the Stock door/anyone exiting it; also covers the most common below-bomb C4 spot for the default plant.

operatorRecommendations.note: Source audio rendered the name phonetically as 'aammy'/'a wii' — matched to Wamai based on the described Mag-NET/magnet gadget play.

generalTips.note: Losing Rafters is the single biggest turning point on this site — attackers get direct access into Cash.

The Rafters player must know exactly how far left/right they can stand without being seen through the breach or CC window ('panel one/two/three'). Panel Two is safest, but attackers know this and target it with fire bolts.
Once the CC/main breach wall opens, defenders on Top Red must stay behind the reinforcement — standing exposed near the edge risks getting feet shot from the Construction door.

Pushing CCTV is easier for attackers than pushing Jacuzzi on the Gym/Bedroom site, due to Gym's reinforcement + rotate combo — ranked attackers tend to favour this site more, though pro play opens Gym/Jacuzzi more often.
Frequently over-picked by players who assume it's the strongest site — Gym and Basement are rated stronger.

reinforcements.note: Same setup pattern as defending the Gym/Bedroom site.
Reinforced, mirroring the Gym/Bedroom setup.
Reinforced.
Reinforced.
Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Flex Wall Near Bar (one of four candidates)
reinforcements.note: Not committed to a specific wall — use it on whichever of four candidate walls near the bar gives an anchor player cover to hold the corridor and Kitchen approach while staying tucked in.

headHoles.location: Top of Bar Wall (Not Fully Destructible Lower Section)
headHoles.note: Sightline straight down to outside the bar door — a busy approach area.

headHoles.location: Cabinet Sightline Toward Construction Door (from upstairs)
headHoles.note: Covers the most common plant spot (behind/near the vending machine) and the second most common plant spot (tucked in the jukebox or far side of a nearby wall).
A tighter line of sight, deliberately not extended too far across.

rotates.location: Long Wall Rotate (Upstairs, Over the Feet Holes)
rotates.note: Same setup as the Gym/Bedroom site — rotate on this wall, feet holes underneath.
Placed so the mattress shields you from the Master/Gym window when using it — do not place on the exposed side.

hatches.note: Deliberately opened so defenders can stop attackers coming through the stock walls; attackers will typically push through the Stock door instead.

If sight control on the upper lines of sight is lost, drop down, use Main Stairs, and reclaim sight into the site without forcing a door entry — pick off attackers through the head holes on the way back in.

hatches.location: Billiard-Area Hatch (holds the billiard window from above)
hatches.note: Only viable while the billiard window stays closed — once attackers open it, holding this hatch position becomes unsafe.

hatches.location: Hatches at Both Ends of the Site
hatches.note: Both ends have a hatch, giving a drop-back escape option from either side if defenders are pushed off site.

gadgetPlacements.note: Head holes only, no reinforcement — a reinforcement here is wasted value since defenders won't hold this spot for long against Cash-side pressure.

gadgetPlacements.location: Strip (Long Sightline to the Stock Double Door)
gadgetPlacements.note: Hold with an ACOG-style scope; a DMR operator (e.g. Rooney/Thunderbird-type pick) could also work.

Use the steps to peek under a Castle barricade and spot attacker toes; play further back or behind the bar rather than right up against the barricade — up close your head lines up with where attackers shoot through the bottom of the door.

operatorRecommendations.note: Holds the long sightline to the stock double door with an ACOG/ACS12 scope; a DMR operator is a viable alternative.
Barricade at the billiard steps to safely spot attacker feet before they can push in.

generalTips.note: Considered the worst site on the map, but can be strong with a fully coordinated 5-stack, mainly because most attackers don't know how to approach it well.
Defending upstairs is imperative — losing the upper lines of sight makes the site very hard to hold.
Playing directly on-site as a static anchor is exposed from multiple angles; hold from range using the upstairs lines of sight and drop back in via the hatches when needed.
Feet-hole reminder: if you see toes through a feet hole, shoot at head height, not the toes.
